content.text: The dice are the identity. Read the accuracy die and you already know the weapon.

A weapon supplies three things: the accuracy die you roll to attack, the damage dice a hit feeds into the pipeline, and the AP each attack costs. How the attack resolves — Evasion, punch-through, tags — lives on Attacks & the Shell Clock.
Which stat swings the weapon: the class decides. The combat pages send you here; this table is the answer.

rows: Light melee (weight 1 — knife, stiletto, precision blade)
Standard & heavy melee (weight 2–3 — longsword, axe, maul)
the beast's own block — see Influence

content.text: One law, printed once: a small accuracy die means a heavy hitter — fewer hits, bigger payloads, and a high crit rate per swing. A big accuracy die means light and reliable. The dice are the identity.
The catalog

rows: innate Penetration 2; the maul with a scope

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: This page owns weapon prices, but the Phase 7 docs never print them — no source table carries a cost column for weapons. Every other catalog on this branch is priced; the weapon table is not.
Reading the table
Accuracy is the die you roll to attack. Damage is what a hit rolls into the pipeline (see Attacks & the Shell Clock).
AP is the cost per attack — and the RP cost of a Reaction Attack with that weapon (see Turn, AP & Reactions).
Weight feeds exactly one rule: Dual Swing's Evasion penalty is the combined weight of both weapons (see Dual Wield & Titan Grip).
Range is printed in band names (see Bands & Movement). Melee weapons reach Adjacent.
Tags — Pierce, Impact, Penetration — are defined once, on Attacks & the Shell Clock.
Heavy weapons (4–5 AP) are two-handed. One-handing one is Titan Grip territory (see Dual Wield & Titan Grip).
Crits
A natural maximum on your accuracy die is a crit: the attack auto-hits and the weapon's own crit effects fire (see Graze & Crits).

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: Graze & Crits sends readers here for each weapon's crit payout, and the master consolidation prices crit effects in beats — but the Phase 7 docs never print a per-weapon crit line. No weapon in any source table carries crit effects. Until the beat budgets are spent per weapon, a crit is an auto-hit and nothing more printable.
Firing penalties
Firing into Adjacent: −2 accuracy with any gun except a sidearm or scattergun. This is the penalty the Bayonet Lug mod deletes (see Mods).
if you're aiming a rifle at something you could spit on, the mistake happened a while ago.
Shots to the Extreme band: −2 accuracy, baseline. Precision's entry augment exists to delete that line (see Precision).
The doctrine matrix
Flesh, Hide, and Shell are defined on Attacks & the Shell Clock. This is what each weapon class is worth against them.

rows: flesh only; the shell never wears
the universal language — toxins don't care about Value

content.text: the matrix is correct. the thing in front of you hasn't read it.
Guns are not free to run: standard resupply and specialty rounds live on Consumables & Ammo. Every weapon has 2 mod slots — the weapon mod catalog lives on Mods.
Weapon classes are assigned by actuarial outcome, not by mass. Carry what your premium allows.
